title: knema tenuinervia subsp. kanburiensis
intro: Knema tenuinervia subsp. kanburiensis, also known as the Kanburi nut, is a subspecies of the Knema tenuinervia plant, which is a species of plant in the family Myristicaceae. It is native to Thailand and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The Kanburi nut has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, and has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the Kanburi nut include decoctions and infusions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and other medicinal products.
